# 02 — WS client must use backend UUID, not readable session ID

Status: ready-for-agent
Labels: bug

*This was generated by AI during triage.*

## What

The backend relay at `/ws/sessions/{id}` calls `UUID.fromString(...)` on the last path segment (`SessionRelayHandler.java:76`). The frontend URL uses human-readable IDs like `abc-defg-hij`. Any WebSocket connection from the frontend is immediately closed with `POLICY_VIOLATION` because the ID fails UUID parsing.

## Root cause

`YjsProvider` receives `roomName` (the URL-readable ID) and will pass it to `createWsProvider`. The backend relay expects the backend-generated UUID for that session.

## Requirements

- The backend UUID is already stored in `localStorage` under key `parchie:{sessionId}:bid` by the bootstrap effect in `EditorPage.tsx`
- Pass the backend UUID as the room name when constructing the WS provider
- The simplest approach: read `localStorage.getItem(`parchie:${sessionId}:bid`)` in `EditorPageInner` and pass it as a separate prop to `YjsProvider`, or hold off creating the provider until the backend UUID is known
- Do not connect until the backend UUID is available — connecting with a null/wrong ID and reconnecting later causes duplicate presence entries

## Acceptance

- Open two tabs at the same `/s/:id` URL
- Both tabs connect to the relay at `/ws/sessions/{uuid}` (verify in browser DevTools → Network → WS)
- Backend logs show two connections for the same session UUID
- No `POLICY_VIOLATION` close frames

## Files

- `src/lib/YjsContext.tsx` (frontend repo) — where provider is created
- `src/pages/EditorPage.tsx` (frontend repo) — where backend UUID is known
- `src/main/java/com/parchie/websocket/SessionRelayHandler.java` (backend) — UUID parsing at line 76, no change needed
